name: Old Country Sourdough Rye Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, sprinkle yeast over 1/3 cup lukewarm water. Stir in 1 teaspoon honey until dissolved.
2. Mix together 1 cup lukewarm water, 1 tablespoon molasses, 1 tablespoon oil, and 1/2 cup mashed potatoes in a large mixing bowl until smooth.
3. Add the yeast mixture, onion starter, caraway seeds, and salt to the wet ingredients. Stir until well combined.
4. Gradually add 2 1/2 cups whole-wheat flour to the mixture, reserving 1/2 cup for kneading. Mix until a dough forms.
5. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 10 minutes, using 1/2 cup of the whole-wheat flour, until smooth and elastic.
6. Place the dough in an oiled bowl, turning it to coat the surface with oil. Cover and let rise at room temperature for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
7. Punch down the dough and divide into two equal portions. Shape each portion into rounds and place on an oiled baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal, or shape them into loaf pans.
8. Brush tops of the loaves with beaten egg and sprinkle with caraway seeds.
9. Let the shaped loaves rise in a cool oven for about 1 hour.
10. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Bake for 20 minutes at 400°F, then reduce temperature to 375°F (190°C) and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes or until golden brown and the bread sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom.
11. Remove from the oven and let cool on a rack before serving.
